task_completion.description: Verify that the data is loaded and the report page is correctly named.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Data/Fields pane on the right shows 'sales_data' loaded. The page tab at the bottom of the canvas is named 'Category Comparison'.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of a Line Chart split into small multiples by Product Category.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A line chart visual is present on the canvas showing 'Sales_Amount' over 'Sale_Date'. It must be split into a grid of smaller line charts (small multiples) labeled with Product Categories (e.g., Clothing, Furniture, Food, Electronics).

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of a Clustered Bar Chart split into small multiples by Customer Type.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A clustered bar chart visual is present showing 'Sales_Amount' by 'Region' (North, West, East, South). It must be split into smaller bar charts (small multiples) labeled with Customer Types (Returning, New).

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the 'Avg_Sale_Value' DAX measure.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Data/Fields pane, there is a field named 'Avg_Sale_Value' with a calculator icon next to it, indicating it is a measure. If selected, the formula bar should show 'DIVIDE(SUM(sales_data[Sales_Amount]), COUNTROWS(sales_data))'.

task_completion.description: Verify the Card visual displaying the Avg_Sale_Value measure.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Card visual (displaying a single large number) is present on the canvas, and its label indicates it is showing 'Avg_Sale_Value'.

task_completion.description: Verify the report is saved with the correct name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Power BI Desktop window title bar reads 'Small_Multiples_Report - Power BI Desktop', or the file 'Small_Multiples_Report.pbix' is visible on the Desktop.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Power BI Desktop GUI to build the report.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Power BI Desktop application open with active interactions (e.g., dragging fields, clicking visual icons), rather than a terminal window running a script to generate the file.

integrity.description: Verify the visuals are actual Power BI charts and not mockups.
integrity.visual_evidence: The charts should have standard Power BI tooltips, axes, and formatting. They should not be static images or text boxes pasted onto the canvas.

integrity.description: Verify the charts render the actual dataset values.
integrity.visual_evidence: The small multiples should specifically show the categories from the dataset (Clothing, Furniture, Food, Electronics for products; Returning, New for customers; North, West, East, South for regions).

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset contains 1000 rows. Expected Product Categories for the line chart small multiples: Clothing, Furniture, Food, Electronics. Expected Customer Types for the bar chart small multiples: Returning, New. Expected Regions: North, West, East, South. The DAX measure must be exactly: DIVIDE(SUM(sales_data[Sales_Amount]), COUNTROWS(sales_data)). The report must be saved as 'Small_Multiples_Report.pbix' and the page must be named 'Category Comparison'.
